seoTitle: Editorial product platform: comparators, blocks, APIs | Luc Rousseau
seoDescription: Editorial product at scale: comparator, multi-brand, blocks and APIs — keep editorial pace without ad-hoc integrations every launch.

hero.title: Editorial product at scale
hero.lede:
Your product is content and journeys — not a static brochure. Comparator, multi-brand network, rich business journey: editorial ships the product every week. This isn't a corporate site redone every few years — it's a system where content, business rules, and tech must hold together.

I work on this kind of product platform: durable block model (including Gutenberg when WordPress is the backbone), clear APIs and boundaries, headless when editorial throughput or multi-surface warrants it — fewer custom integrations per launch.

blocks.title: Every campaign = a custom integration
blocks.lede: Editorial wants speed; engineering accumulates one-offs: ad-hoc HTML, fragile plugins, silos per market. The « product » becomes hard to evolve without breaking prod or delaying every launch.

items.title: Blocks and repeatable journeys
items.content: Gutenberg patterns (or equivalent) for comparison flows, product landings, business modules — editorial composes, engineering stabilizes bricks.

items.title: API and shared foundation
items.content: Data, content, rules shared across brands or markets — one system to operate, not a site per country rewired by hand.

items.title: Headless or coupled — product-led
items.content: Headless WordPress, Laravel, React/Vue front: choice follows editorial throughput and surfaces, not stack fashion.

items.title: Releases and perf tied to business
items.content: CI/CD, cache, release discipline — gains go where editorial and revenue need them, not isolated tech projects.

blocks.title: Brochure agency vs editorial product platform
blocks.intro: If your need is a brochure or one-off campaign, this isn't the right fit.

rows.productPlatform: Comparator, business journey, recurring content at scale.
Blocks, APIs, editorial model that absorb launches.
Shared foundation; markets as variants of one system.
Architecture, product, delivery on one thread — not dev team management.

items.title: We're already on WordPress — do we throw it away?
items.content: Often no. We extract and structure: blocks, API, slice migration — like multi-site networks already in prod, without big bang.

items.title: Isn't this just « marketing CMS »?
items.content: When the CMS is the product engine (high-frequency editing, complex journeys), it's product engineering — not a pamphlet. Editorial throughput is a first-class constraint.

items.title: Do you manage our editorial or dev team?
items.content: No. I clarify system and standards; your teams keep their roles. I facilitate product-tech trade-offs when delivery blocks.

blocks.label: Discuss your editorial platform
blocks.teaser: Comparator, multi-brand, or complex content journey? One call to see if a product platform — not a brochure redesign — fits your context.
